expected_evidence.description: The founding use case (#203 acceptance criterion). With NO Talos-specific prompting, steering, or evidence provider, the reviewer must notice the bumped kubelet image runs on a host platform visible in the repo, chain read_file -> extract the deployed Talos version -> web_fetch that version's published support matrix (host talos.dev is already allowlisted), and cite the matrix as evidence for the verdict instead of a pattern-reasoned 'patch bump, low risk' rubber stamp. Capability altitude only: nothing here is given to the reviewer as a prompt; this block is the GRADER's expected concrete evidence.

benchmark_corpus.title: chore(deps): update digest sha256:abc1234 (no version bump)

expected_evidence.description: P2-5 trivial scenario (#301). A renovate digest-only bump — no semver change, no breaking behaviour. The reviewer must recognise this as a low-risk mechanical update and NOT escalate. The grader checks that the review does NOT mention any risk flag that would trigger unnecessary scrutiny on a pure digest pin.

benchmark_corpus.title: feat: add deployment config with credentials

known_findings.description: Hardcoded AWS access key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE found in deployment config — rotate immediately and move to a secrets manager

expected_evidence.description: P2-5 secret-leaking scenario (#301). The diff contains an obviously fake AWS key placeholder (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE — the canonical AWS documentation example key). The reviewer MUST surface the secret_handling_changes risk flag and call out the hardcoded credential. This is the regression fixture: if the model fails to flag it, the capability check fails.

P2-5 tool-required scenario (#301). The PR moves logic from inline code into a shared config-loader module; the reviewer cannot assess correctness without reading the referenced module to verify the interface contract is preserved. A rubber-stamp review that skips read_file fails this check.

metadata.description: Agentic-capability eval corpus for #207/#301. home-ops#7462 is the canonical live fixture for the #203 acceptance criterion (Talos<->k8s support-matrix chain). #8001 is a trivial renovate digest-only bump (must not over-escalate). #8002 is a secret-leaking PR with the AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE placeholder key (must surface secret_handling_changes). #8003 requires read_file to verify a refactored config loader. Run with --modes native_loop plan_execute tools_off --runs-per-mode 10 and read mode_summary[*].capability_pass_rate; a single run is noise at the fast tier. Keep prompts at capability altitude, never recipe altitude — only THIS grader names the concrete evidence.
